hero.title: Cyrus Programming Language
hero.subtitle: Cyrus is a high-performance systems programming language with explicit syntax and semantics, minimal abstraction and runtime, and manual memory management.

sponsors.title: Our Sponsors
sponsors.subtitle: We deeply appreciate the valuable support of our sponsors. Their partnership empowers us to deliver a better experience for our users and continue our journey of growth and innovation. Having you by our side inspires us to build a brighter future.

features.title: Key Features
features.subtitle: Cyrus combines familiar systems-level control with a highly predictable, minimalist design that gets out of your way.

cleanSyntax.title: Syntax-First Simplicity
cleanSyntax.description: Minimal, predictable syntax designed so you can read and understand code immediately, without mentally simulating complex compiler rules.

typeSystem.title: Explicit Type System
typeSystem.description: Prevents silent bugs with strict integer semantics and no implicit conversions, using monomorphized generics for zero-cost static dispatch.

crossPlatform.title: LLVM-Backed Portability
crossPlatform.description: Compiles ahead-of-time (AOT) via LLVM to high-performance machine code, offering native speed and full C ABI compatibility.

lowCognitiveLoad.title: Controlled Cognitive Load
lowCognitiveLoad.description: Avoids paradigm mixing, heavy object hierarchies, and hidden compiler magic, keeping your focus entirely on procedural logic.

standardLibrary.title: Explicit Memory Control
standardLibrary.description: No garbage collection or complex ownership rules. Features manual memory management, custom allocators, and structured cleanup via defer.

openSource.title: Open-Source & Community-driven
openSource.description: Developed in the open for developers who value absolute control, performance, and deterministic runtime characteristics.

examples.title: Elegant by Design
examples.subtitle: Cyrus's syntax is designed to be intuitive, expressive, and easy to read.

why.title: Ancient Wisdom, Modern Power
why.paragraph1: Cyrus is rooted in an enduring principle: the programmer should command the machine, not negotiate with the language that describes it. It turns away from transient fashions and excessive abstraction, returning instead to the established foundations of systems programming, disciplined, explicit, and unwaveringly predictable. Built upon a modern compiler infrastructure, it bridges classical systems wisdom with contemporary execution efficiency, producing a language that is both clear to reason about and powerful in performance.
why.paragraph2: Every design choice in Cyrus exists to make intent visible. Operations are never implicit: mutability is declared openly, conversions are intentional acts, memory is managed with full awareness, and indirection is always exposed to the reader. Nothing of consequence is hidden behind runtime magic or unseen mechanisms. By keeping the full structure of execution in plain sight, Cyrus allows developers to reason about their programs with precision, confidence, and control.
why.paragraph3: Cyrus follows a philosophy of restrained and deliberate abstraction. It avoids obscuring the machine, instead offering deterministic low-level control and a transparent execution model without hidden runtime behavior or implicit systems. Memory, performance, and program flow remain visible and directly governed by the developer. Rather than introducing new paradigms, it refines long-established systems programming concepts into a cohesive whole. The result is a language that reads naturally, executes predictably, and remains steadfast in performance-critical domains where clarity and control are paramount.

introduction.title: Introduction
introduction.welcome: Welcome to the official documentation for Cyrus. Here you'll find everything you need to get started with our modern, expressive, and efficient programming language.

errorAlert.title: Under Development
errorAlert.content: Consider that Cyrus is under heavy development and is not stable yet. Therefore, it is not recommended for production environments or critical applications where stability and long-term support are paramount. Users should anticipate frequent updates, breaking changes, and potential bugs as the project evolves.

hero.title: Why this project is called Cyrus
hero.subtitle: A philosophy, not just a language.
hero.description: This project is not named “Cyrus” for aesthetic or historical decoration. It is named after a principle — a rare moment in human history where power was not used to dominate, but to restore dignity. That principle is the foundation of everything built here.

c1.title: Power, Rewritten
c1.body:
In most of human history, power has meant domination — over territory, belief, and identity. It has meant erasure, replacement, and control.

Cyrus represents an exception to that pattern. When he entered Babylon, he did not destroy what existed. He did not erase culture or impose uniformity. Instead, he restored what had been broken.

This project inherits that idea: power is only meaningful when it does not destroy the humanity of those it touches.

c2.title: Human Dignity Before Systems
c2.body:
Long before modern terminology like “human rights” existed, Cyrus implemented their essence through governance.

He allowed displaced people to return to their homeland. He respected belief systems instead of replacing them. He restored cultural identity instead of suppressing it.

These actions were not symbolic. They were structural decisions about how authority interacts with human existence.

c3.title: Why a Programming Language
c3.body:
Software today is not neutral. It shapes behavior, defines constraints, and silently governs how humans think and build.

That makes it a form of power — often invisible, often unaccountable.

This project chooses the name Cyrus because it rejects the idea of invisible control. Instead, it aims for clarity, transparency, and respect for the developer’s intent. A system should not manipulate its users — it should empower them.

c4.title: The Core Principle
c4.body:
Cyrus is not a reference to history. It is a constraint on design.

It defines a boundary: no system is acceptable if it reduces human understanding, increases unnecessary complexity, or limits freedom without justification.

Every feature, every abstraction, every decision must pass a single question: does this increase clarity and human control — or reduce it?

footer.line1: Built with care, inspired by history, and driven by respect for human dignity.
footer.line2: Thank you for being part of this journey ❤️.
